zele.label: Gmail
zele.capabilities: Read inbox, search/send/reply email, email addresses — multi-account Gmail, OAuth browser login for setup.
zele.description: Use for anything Gmail: reading the user's inbox, searching mail, sending or replying to email. Multi-account, OAuth login via `zele login`.

xurl.label: X
xurl.capabilities: Post tweets, reply & quote, read timelines, search tweets, like & repost, follow/unfollow, DMs, media uploads
xurl.description: Use to interact with X/Twitter: post/reply/quote tweets, search, read timelines, like/repost, follow, DMs, media upload. Authenticated raw API access via `xurl <method> <path>`.

tiktok-uploader.label: TikTok
tiktok-uploader.capabilities: Upload videos, batch uploads, schedule posts, custom covers, hashtags & mentions
tiktok-uploader.description: Upload videos to TikTok, schedule posts, set covers and hashtags.

instagrapi.label: Instagram
instagrapi.capabilities: Upload photos & reels, post stories, send DMs, like & comment, manage followers
instagrapi.description: Instagram automation: upload photos/reels, stories, DMs, likes, follower management.

mudslide.label: WhatsApp
mudslide.capabilities: Send messages, send images & files, send locations & polls, group management, QR code login
mudslide.description: Use to send WhatsApp messages/files/media/locations/polls from CLI. QR-code login via `mudslide login`.

telegram-send.label: Telegram
telegram-send.capabilities: Send messages, send files & images, send video & audio, Markdown/HTML formatting, channel & group support
telegram-send.description: Send Telegram messages and files from CLI via a bot.

apollo-api.label: Apollo
apollo-api.capabilities: Lead search & enrichment, email sequences, CRM sync
apollo-api.description: Use for B2B prospecting: search Apollo's people/company DB, enrich leads with emails/titles/companies, manage sequences and CRM data. Needs APOLLO_API_KEY.

meta-ads.label: Meta Ads
meta-ads.capabilities: Meta/Facebook & Instagram ad campaigns: campaigns/adsets/ads CRUD, insights & reporting, catalog/product-feed/product-set/product-item, ad creatives, datasets (pixels), pages — Marketing API via the official `meta` CLI.
meta-ads.description: Manage Meta/Facebook & Instagram ad campaigns, insights, creatives, and catalogs.

elevenlabs-api.label: ElevenLabs
elevenlabs-api.description: Use to generate speech audio from text, clone voices, or list voices. Needs ELEVENLABS_API_KEY.

codex-imagegen-api.label: Image Gen
codex-imagegen-api.capabilities: AI image generation & editing via TODOFORAI backend (gpt-image)
codex-imagegen-api.description: Generate or edit images with AI.

suno-api.label: Suno
suno-api.capabilities: AI music generation, custom lyrics, multiple genres
suno-api.description: Use to generate music/songs from a prompt or custom lyrics. Needs SUNO_API_KEY.

ntn.label: Notion
ntn.capabilities: Official Notion CLI: workspace-wide OAuth login, raw API calls (`ntn api <path>`), page/datasource management, file uploads, Workers deploy.
ntn.description: Read, create, and update Notion pages and databases.

perplexity-api.label: Perplexity
perplexity-api.capabilities: AI-powered web search, chat completions, async chat, embeddings and agent runs
perplexity-api.description: Web-grounded AI search with citations. Use when you need fresh information beyond training cutoff.

gh.label: GitHub
gh.capabilities: Repos, PRs & issues, actions & releases, gists & SSH keys
gh.description: Use for any GitHub operation: create/list/merge PRs, file issues, manage releases, trigger/inspect Actions, clone/fork repos, gists. Prefer `gh` over raw GitHub API calls.

glab.label: GitLab
glab.capabilities: Repos, MRs & issues, CI/CD pipelines, releases
glab.description: Use for any GitLab operation: MRs, issues, pipelines, releases, repo management. GitLab equivalent of `gh`.

vercel.label: Vercel
vercel.capabilities: Deploy & preview, environment variables, domain management
vercel.description: Use to deploy a project to Vercel (`vercel deploy`), manage env vars, domains, and preview URLs.

netlify.label: Netlify
netlify.capabilities: Deploy & preview, serverless functions, forms & identity
netlify.description: Use to deploy sites/functions to Netlify, manage env vars, forms, identity. `netlify deploy --prod` for production.

firebase.label: Firebase
firebase.capabilities: Hosting & deploy, Firestore & Realtime DB, auth & functions
firebase.description: Use for Firebase: deploy hosting/functions, manage Firestore/Realtime DB rules and data, auth users, emulators.

wrangler.label: Cloudflare
wrangler.capabilities: Workers & Pages deploy, KV & D1 storage, R2 & Queues
wrangler.description: Use for Cloudflare developer platform: deploy Workers/Pages, manage KV/D1/R2/Queues, tail logs. Use `cloudflared` for tunnels.

stripe.label: Stripe
stripe.capabilities: Payments & subscriptions, webhook testing, invoice management
stripe.description: Use for Stripe: inspect/create customers, charges, subscriptions, invoices, webhooks. `stripe listen` to forward webhooks locally; `stripe get/post /v1/...` for raw API.

flyctl.label: Fly.io
flyctl.statusCmd: flyctl auth whoami 2>&1 | grep -v 'not logged in' | head -1
flyctl.capabilities: Deploy containers globally, Postgres & volumes, auto-scaling
flyctl.description: Use to deploy apps/containers to Fly.io edge, manage Postgres/volumes/machines/secrets. `flyctl deploy` from app dir.

supabase.label: Supabase
supabase.capabilities: Postgres database, auth & storage, edge functions
supabase.description: Use for Supabase projects: run/migrate local Postgres, deploy edge functions, manage auth/storage, link to cloud project.

railway.label: Railway
railway.capabilities: Deploy apps & databases, environment management, auto-scaling
railway.description: Use to deploy apps/services to Railway, provision databases, manage env vars and environments. `railway up` to deploy current dir.

shopify.label: Shopify
shopify.capabilities: Theme development, app scaffolding, store management
shopify.description: Use for Shopify dev: scaffold/develop themes and apps, push/pull themes, run dev server against a store.

datadog-ci.label: Datadog
datadog-ci.capabilities: CI test visibility, sourcemap uploads, deployment tracking
datadog-ci.description: Use in CI to upload test results/sourcemaps/coverage to Datadog and mark deployments. Needs DATADOG_API_KEY.

sentry-cli.label: Sentry
sentry-cli.statusCmd: sentry-cli info 2>&1 | grep -q 'Authentication Info' && echo authenticated
sentry-cli.capabilities: Release management, sourcemap uploads, error monitoring
sentry-cli.description: Use to create Sentry releases, upload sourcemaps/debug-symbols, associate commits, send events. `sentry-cli releases new <version>`.

todoai.label: TODOforAI
todoai.capabilities: Create/list/inspect/update TODOs, run templates & workflows, platform API access
todoai.description: Use for the TODOforAI platform — this is how you reach the user's OWN TODOs: `todoai list` (filter with `--status open`) to browse them, `todoai "prompt"` to create one, `todoai --inspect <id>` to read a TODO's full chat log, `todoai status/addmessage/delete` to manage them, `--template` to run a registry workflow. Never claim you lack access to platform TODOs — reach them here; run `--help` for details.

newman.label: Postman
newman.capabilities: Run API test collections, CI/CD integration, HTML reports
newman.description: Use to run Postman collections from CLI (API smoke tests, CI checks). `newman run <collection.json> -e <env.json>`.

curl.label: Web Request
curl.capabilities: HTTP/HTTPS/FTP client: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE, headers, auth, file upload/download, follow redirects, cookies, TLS.

cloudflared.label: Tunnel
cloudflared.capabilities: Tunnel to localhost, expose local services, zero-trust access
cloudflared.description: Use to expose a local port to the public internet via a Cloudflare tunnel. Quick: `cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:<port>` prints a public https URL.

vault.label: HashiCorp Vault
vault.description: HashiCorp Vault CLI — only for Terraform/vals/ecosystem integrations that expect it. For all credential read/write tasks use `tfa-vault` instead. Needs VAULT_ADDR + VAULT_TOKEN.

rclone.label: Cloud Sync
rclone.statusCmd: rclone listremotes 2>&1 | grep -v 'NOTICE' | grep -q ':' && echo authenticated
rclone.capabilities: Access Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, S3 and 40+ cloud providers. List, copy, sync, move, mount files as virtual filesystem (FUSE). Use 'rclone config create <name> <provider>' to connect (opens browser for OAuth).
rclone.description: Access cloud storage (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, S3, 40+ providers). List, copy, sync, or mount files.

gdrive.label: Google Drive
gdrive.userInfoCmd: TOKEN=$(rclone config dump 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.loads(json.load(sys.stdin)['{{REMOTE}}']['token'])['access_token'])") && curl -sf 'https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/about?fields=user' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['user']['emailAddress'])" 2>/dev/null

onedrive.label: OneDrive
onedrive.userInfoCmd: TOKEN=$(rclone config dump 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.loads(json.load(sys.stdin)['{{REMOTE}}']['token'])['access_token'])") && curl -sf 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('mail') or d.get('userPrincipalName',''))" 2>/dev/null

dropbox.label: Dropbox
dropbox.userInfoCmd: TOKEN=$(rclone config dump 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.loads(json.load(sys.stdin)['{{REMOTE}}']['token'])['access_token'])") && curl -sf -X POST 'https://api.dropboxapi.com/2/users/get_current_account' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['email'])" 2>/dev/null

pymupdf.label: PDF
pymupdf.capabilities: PDF text editing, extraction, merge, split
pymupdf.description: Read, edit, merge, or split PDFs programmatically. Call via `python3 -c`.

agent-browser.label: Browser
agent-browser.capabilities: Headless browser automation, web scraping, accessibility tree snapshots, screenshots, PDF generation
agent-browser.description: Headless browser. Use for JS-rendered pages, scraping, screenshots, PDFs, and automated flows. Default browser — prefer over `todoforai-browser` unless the user's own session is needed.

todoforai-browser.label: Browser (Extension)
todoforai-browser.capabilities: Browser automation via extension (non-headless), web scraping, accessibility tree snapshots, screenshots, PDF generation
todoforai-browser.description: Drives the user's real browser via the extension. Use when the task needs the user's actual logged-in session, cookies, or MFA.

todoforai-subagent.label: Sub-agent
todoforai-subagent.capabilities: Spawn a TODO for AI sub-agent (FluidAgent) from the CLI; pipe stdin in, get an answer out
todoforai-subagent.description: Spawn a sub-agent for a focused task. Pipe context via stdin.

todoforai-summary.label: Summarize (Lite)
todoforai-summary.capabilities: Summarize files or piped input via a sub-agent
todoforai-summary.description: Summarize files or piped content via a sub-agent.

tfa-explore.label: Explore
tfa-explore.capabilities: Explore a codebase as a real TODO: read-only agent maps structure, surfaces relevant files, streams findings to terminal
tfa-explore.description: Explore a codebase with a read-only sub-agent. Streams findings live.

tfa-review.label: Review
tfa-review.capabilities: Review a git diff as a real TODO: read-only agent assesses goal, finds issues, suggests simpler approaches
tfa-review.description: Review a git diff with a read-only sub-agent.

tfa-summary.label: Summarize
tfa-summary.capabilities: Summarize files or piped input as a real TODO with no-tools sub-agent
tfa-summary.description: Summarize files or piped content as a visible TODO.

tfa-vault.label: Vault
tfa-vault.capabilities: Zero-config TODOforAI vault CLI: put/get/patch/list/rm secrets in the user's KV v2 vault. Reuses bridge credentials (TODOFORAI_API_KEY / ~/.config/todoforai/credentials.json); derives vault URL from backend URL.
tfa-vault.description: The user's secret store. Always check here first for API keys, tokens, credentials, bank cards, and private/secret account details before looking elsewhere.

ripgrep.label: Search
ripgrep.capabilities: Fast recursive grep with regex, .gitignore-aware, parallel, unicode-correct

fd.label: Find Files
fd.capabilities: Fast user-friendly find replacement: regex by default, .gitignore-aware, parallel

jq.label: JSON
jq.capabilities: Command-line JSON processor: query, filter, transform, format JSON streams

patch.label: Apply Diff
patch.capabilities: Apply unified/context diff patches to files; reverse, dry-run, fuzzy matching

sed.label: Replace Text
sed.capabilities: Stream editor: in-place text substitution, line filtering, scripted edits

grep.label: Search Text
grep.capabilities: Search plain text for lines matching a regex: recursive, fixed-string, context, invert, count
